chapters.title: — Cold Open: The Clinical Question That Moved
— Headline: Clinical Question and Why a Meta-Analysis Earns the Grade
— Headline: Effect Size and the Pre-Selection Gradient
— Round 4: Kim DeLuca / cfDNA and SCA Detection

follow_up_links.reason: Provides pooled evidence for sequencing yield over CMA in structurally abnormal fetuses, directly informing when to offer exome or genome sequencing
follow_up_links.title: Diagnostic Yield of Sequencing for Prenatal Diagnosis of Fetal Structural Anomalies: An Updated Systematic Review

follow_up_links.reason: Demonstrates that single-cell trophoblast sequencing can detect submicroscopic CNVs non-invasively, potentially reducing need for amniocentesis
follow_up_links.title: Single-cell-based non-invasive screening for fetal pathogenic microimbalances using maternal blood

follow_up_links.reason: Largest Latin American FETO dataset confirms survival benefit and extends outcome benchmarks beyond European and North American centers
follow_up_links.title: Latin American Multicenter Study of Fetal Endoscopic Tracheal Occlusion for Severe Left Diaphragmatic Hernia

follow_up_links.reason: Largest early-TTTS laser series provides stage-specific survival data for counseling families facing intervention before 18 weeks
follow_up_links.title: Perinatal outcomes of twin pregnancies complicated by early twin-to-twin transfusion syndrome treated with fetoscopic laser surgery

follow_up_links.reason: Population-level evidence that cfDNA screening has shifted SCA detection earlier, changing the timing and burden of counseling and subspecialty referral
follow_up_links.title: Impact of Prenatal Cell-Free DNA Screening on Sex Chromosome Aneuploidy Diagnoses in Infants and Children

key_claims.claim: Sequencing adds a 27% incremental diagnostic yield over CMA in structurally abnormal fetuses, rising to 40% in phenotypically pre-selected cohorts
Single-cell trophoblast sequencing from maternal blood detects pathogenic CNVs including microimbalances under 8 Mb that cfDNA misses
FETO for severe left CDH demonstrates survival benefit over expectant management in a propensity-matched Latin American multicenter cohort
Fetoscopic laser for early TTTS at or before 18 weeks yields stage-stratified survival rates in the largest multicenter series to date
Introduction of prenatal cfDNA screening was associated with an immediate and sustained increase in SCA diagnoses in infants and children
